title: codonoboea castaneifolia
intro: Codonoboea castaneifolia, also known as the Forest Mahogany or Mahogany tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Malaysia and Indonesia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Codonoboea castaneifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support immune system function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction for its herbal benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
